All about aRboretum

aRboretum: Complete technical guide for botanical collections

Martin Boucknooghe & Domingos Cardoso

2026-06-17

Source: vignettes/aRboretum.Rmd
aRboretum.Rmd

Introduction

aRboretum is an integrated R package designed to automate the production of botanical collection labels, from taxonomic data extraction to interactive web labels and print-ready QR codes. It can be used for arboreta, botanic gardens, educational trails, and other living plant collections.

The package offers:

  • multilingual output in Portuguese, English, French, and Spanish;
  • synonym resolution and integration of data from Flora e Funga do Brasil (FFB) and Plants of the World Online (POWO);
  • interactive HTML labels with species information, text-to-speech, distribution maps, photo slideshows, and optional personal audio recordings;
  • a searchable minisite index linking all generated labels;
  • printable QR-code labels in different layouts;
  • one flexible additional language slot for community-based accessibility through add_lang.

All functions are designed to work together. This vignette provides a technical overview of the package workflow, main arguments, expected data structure, and common customisation options.

Installation 

# Install from GitHub
if (!require("devtools")) install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("DBOSlab/aRboretum")

Main dependencies are installed automatically with the package.

Internal data structures

The internal translation dictionary

The package contains an internal translation table used to build species descriptions in the built-in languages. It includes terms and sentence fragments related to:

  • taxonomy;
  • endemism and establishment means;
  • phytogeographic domains and vegetation types;
  • IUCN categories;
  • genus diversity notes;
  • sentence-building connectors.

The helper functions .tr_dict() and .phrase_generator() use this dictionary to produce natural-language species summaries.

Botanical regions mapping

The package also relies on an internal mapping between botanical division codes, countries, and continents. This allows distribution data from POWO to be converted into readable geographic statements.

Data columns expected

Expected data structure

Most functions rely on a standard data frame generated by arboretum_data(). Typical columns include:

family
taxonName
scientificNameAuthorship
FFB.vernacularName
country
endemism
FFB.establishmentMeans
FFB.stateProvince
FFB.phytogeographicDomain
FFB.vegetationType
botanical_country
introduced_to
IUCN.status
plant_uses_EN
plant_uses_PT
plant_uses_ES
plant_uses_FR
free_notes_EN
free_notes_PT
free_notes_ES
free_notes_FR
POWO.url
FFB.url
full_phrases_ADD_LANGUAGE

Not every column needs to be filled, but the core taxonomic and distribution fields are required for full functionality.

Main workflow

A typical aRboretum workflow consists of the following steps:

  1. extract and structure species data with arboretum_data();
  2. optionally prepare folders for personal audio with arboretum_audios();
  3. optionally prepare folders for species photos with arboretum_photos();
  4. generate interactive HTML labels with arboretum_labels();
  5. create a searchable microsite with arboretum_minisite();
  6. create printable QR-code labels with arboretum_qrcodes().

Function overview

arboretum_data()

What it does

arboretum_data() extracts and merges species information from Flora e Funga do Brasil and Plants of the World Online. It resolves synonyms when possible, merges overlapping information, and returns a standardised dataset that can be used by the rest of the package.

The output also includes empty user-editable columns such as plant_uses_* and free_notes_*, which can later be used to enrich labels in the built-in languages.

Example 

spp_list <- c("Luetzelburgia bahiensis", "Paubrasilia echinata")

extracted_data <- arboretum_data(
  spp_list = spp_list,
  save = TRUE,
  format = "xlsx",
  dir = "arboretum_data"
)

Notes

  • Synonyms are resolved to accepted names when possible.
  • Missing values from one source may still be complemented by the other.
  • The saved output can be edited manually before generating labels.

arboretum_audios()

What it does

arboretum_audios() prepares a folder structure for personal species audio recordings. For each species and each selected language, the function creates one folder where users can place audio files.

It also generates an HTML guide to facilitate the recording workflow.

Example 

arboretum_audios(
  data_path = "arboretum_data/arboretum_data.xlsx",
  printed_lang = c("pt", "en", "fr", "es"),
  add_lang = "PANARA",
  verbose = TRUE
)

Notes

  • Built-in languages are "pt", "en", "fr", and "es".
  • The optional add_lang argument creates one additional language folder per species, such as PANARA.
  • This is useful when preparing personal audio in a community or local language without translating the whole interface.

arboretum_photos()

What it does

arboretum_photos() creates one photo folder per species so that users can add personal or curated images to be embedded later in the HTML labels.

Example 

arboretum_photos(
  data_path = "arboretum_data/arboretum_data.xlsx",
  verbose = TRUE
)

Notes

  • Existing folders are preserved.
  • Photos are optional and only appear in labels when files are found in the expected species folder.

arboretum_labels()

What it does

arboretum_labels() generates one standalone HTML label per species. Each label may include:

  • multilingual species text;
  • taxonomic information;
  • world and Brazil distribution maps;
  • browser-based speech synthesis;
  • personal audio recordings when available;
  • photo slideshows when available;
  • IUCN iconography;
  • institutional and package logos.

Example 

arboretum_labels(
  data_path = "arboretum_data/arboretum_data.xlsx",
  audio_dir = "arboretum_audios",
  photo_dir = "arboretum_photos",
  printed_lang = c("pt", "en"),
  add_lang = "TUKANO",
  path_to_logo = "path/to/institution_logo.png",
  logo_url = "https://www.example.org",
  dir = "species_labels",
  verbose = TRUE
)

The add_lang workflow

The add_lang argument provides a flexible, interim pathway for including one additional community or local language in species labels and associated audio, without requiring translation of the full website interface.

When add_lang is supplied:

  • arboretum_labels() looks for a column named full_phrases_ADD_LANGUAGE;
  • if that column contains non-empty text, the extra language is added as an additional text option in the label;
  • the custom language text is read directly from full_phrases_ADD_LANGUAGE;
  • this extra language does not pass through the standard phrase-generation and translation workflow.

This is especially useful in collaborative work with Indigenous peoples or other local communities, where species-level accessibility may be more important than translating the full minisite interface.

Notes

  • Personal audio files take priority over browser speech synthesis.
  • For custom languages added through add_lang, personal recordings are recommended because browser voices may not be available.
  • Photos and maps are included only when available.

arboretum_minisite()

What it does

arboretum_minisite() builds a searchable index page linking all generated species labels. It can also include simple collection summaries and charts when the species dataset is provided.

Example 

arboretum_minisite(
  labels_dir = "species_labels",
  data_path = "arboretum_data/arboretum_data.xlsx",
  site_title = "Garden Collection",
  printed_lang = c("en", "pt", "es", "fr"),
  logo = "path/to/logo.png",
  logo_url = "https://www.example.org",
  group_by_family = TRUE
)

Notes

  • The interface language order follows the order of printed_lang.
  • The minisite can work without data_path, but summary panels may be reduced.

arboretum_qrcodes()

What it does

arboretum_qrcodes() creates printable QR-code labels that can link to species HTML labels, external URLs, or source records. The output can be generated in minimalist or complete layouts.

Example 

arboretum_qrcodes(
  data_path = "arboretum_data/arboretum_data.xlsx",
  base_url = "https://myinstitution.org/collection",
  layout = "minimalist",
  dir = "qrcodes_mini"
)

arboretum_qrcodes(
  data_path = "arboretum_data/arboretum_data.xlsx",
  base_url = "https://myinstitution.org/collection",
  layout = "complete",
  width = 6.0,
  length = 8.0,
  id_code = "catalogue_number",
  printed_lang = "en",
  path_to_logo = "path/to/logo.png",
  color = "#1a2e1a",
  format = "pdf",
  dir = "qrcodes_complete"
)

Notes

  • When no custom URL is provided, the function falls back to source URLs when possible.
  • Output can span multiple pages automatically.

Practical example with one additional language

The example below illustrates a simple workflow where Portuguese and English remain the main interface languages, while Tukano is added as a community language for species labels and optional personal recordings.

arboretum_audios(
  data_path = "arboretum_data/arboretum_data.xlsx",
  printed_lang = c("pt", "en"),
  add_lang = "TUKANO"
)

arboretum_labels(
  data_path = "arboretum_data/arboretum_data.xlsx",
  audio_dir = "arboretum_audios",
  printed_lang = c("pt", "en"),
  add_lang = "TUKANO",
  dir = "species_labels"
)

For this workflow, the input file should contain a column named full_phrases_ADD_LANGUAGE with the full text to display in Panará for each species.

Defensive checks and internal helpers

Several internal helper functions provide validation and error prevention.

.arg_check_spp_list()

  • trims whitespace;
  • standardises input species names;
  • checks for invalid entries.

.arg_check_dir()

  • validates output directory names;
  • removes trailing slashes when needed.

.arg_check_printed_lang()

  • standardises language codes to lower case;
  • removes duplicates;
  • validates built-in language codes.

.read_species_data()

  • checks file existence and extension;
  • reads CSV or Excel input;
  • validates required columns.

.copy_folder_progress()

  • copies user-provided audio or photo folders into output directories;
  • shows progress feedback when many files are involved.

Phrase-generation helpers

Helpers such as .phrase_generator(), .tr_dict(), .botdiv_to_countries(), and .add_genus_curiosity_notes() support the construction of species descriptions in the built-in languages.

Conclusion

aRboretum provides a practical pipeline for creating modern botanical collection labels, from taxonomic data retrieval to multilingual HTML pages and printable QR codes. The package combines automation with flexibility, allowing users to enrich labels with notes, uses, photos, personal audio, and one additional community-based language through add_lang.

For function-specific details, see the package documentation:

?arboretum_data
?arboretum_audios
?arboretum_photos
?arboretum_labels
?arboretum_minisite
?arboretum_qrcodes

On this page
